rulings.Case Title: VALERIE GORNAIA VS. WELLS FARGO HOME MORTGAGE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Wells Fargo To Provide Further Verified Responses, Without Objection Together With Identification And Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 26, 2013, Line 6: Plaintiff VALERIE GORNAIA'S Motion to Compel Wells Fargo to Provide Further Verified Responses, without Objection Together with Identification and Production of Documents Pro Tem Judge James Weixel, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses is denied. Plaintiff's requests for production of documents and other things did not require defendant to provide voice recordings. Furthermore, there is no requirement for defendant to create documents or identify or re-label them in the manner Plaintiff appears to demand. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to appeals@jimweixel.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PROVIDENCE CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION, A NON PROFIT VS. SPNS GOLDEN GATE, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, September 26, 2013, Line 2, CROSS COMPLAINANTS SPNS GOLDEN GATE, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED, STANDARD PACIFIC CORP.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION The motion is denied. First, the Court sustains the objection to the declaration of Mr. Billy. Mr. Billy fails to authenticate the documents attached to his declaration. Second, the Court determines that the trial judge should rule on the duty to defend issue. Movants are not prejudiced because Zurich Insurance and Steadfast are currently defending them. In addition, denying the motion will avoid the practical difficulties of sorting out multiple, and potentially conflicting, duties to assume the active defense of litigation in progress. See Crawford v. Weather Shield Mfg. Inc. (2008) 44 Cal.4th 541, 565, fn.12. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/LCN)

rulings.Case Title: DIANE CONNELL VS. HAR-BRO OF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA, INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, September 26, 2013, Line 3, DEFENDANT HAR-BRO OF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant's Demurrer to the Second Amended Complaint is overruled. The Court finds that Cal. Penal Code §496(c) permits theft and receipt of stolen property to be pled in the alternative. Plaintiff has pled facts sufficient to establish the three elements necessary for a cause of action for receipt of stolen property: (1) the property was stolen; (2) the defendant knew that the property was stolen; and (3) the defendant had possession of such property. (Castillo-Cruz v. Holder (9th Cir. 2009) 581 F.3d 1154, 1161.) (See Complaint, 131.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/LCN)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTIAN ROETTGERS VS. AUTONET MOBILE, INC. A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, September 26, 2013, Line 4, DEFENDANT EASTON CAPITAL PARTNERS LP'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Motion to strike is granted, with leave to amend. Plaintiff may amend, if he can do so in good faith, to plead specific acts committed by an Easton Capital Partners LP officer, director, or managing agent, as required by Cal. Civ. Code § 3294(B). Plaintiff has otherwise pled malice and/or oppression for the purposes of establishing a punitive damages claim. rulings.Case Title: CHRISTIAN ROETTGERS VS. AUTONET MOBILE, INC. A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, September 26, 2013, Line 4, DEFENDANT EASTON CAPITAL PARTNERS LP'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Demurrer is overruled as to both causes of action. Easton Capital Partners LP is not an agent of Autonet Mobile Inc. under California agency laws, and the business judgment rule is inapplicable as Easton Capital Partners LP is not a director of Autonet Mobile Inc. (Cal. Corp. Code § 309.) The pleadings sufficiently allege that Easton Capital Partners LP intentionally acted to induce a breach of contract and intentionally interfere with a contractual relationship. Pleadings also state that Easton Capital Partners LP's conduct caused the breach and disruption of the contractual relationship. rulings.Case Title: JASON B. OCHOA VS. GEICO INSURANCE CORP.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Dismissal And File An Amended
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, September 26, 2013, Line 20, PLAINTIFF JASON OCHOA'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Dismissal And File An Amended Plaintiff's Motion to Set Aside Dismissal and File an Amended Complaint is denied. Plaintiff cannot state a cause of action against the defendant named in the proposed Second Amended Complaint. In Exhibit I-A attached to the proposed SAC, Plaintiff states the accident occurred on May 5, 2013. The statute of limitations for personal injury claims and property damage claims expired on May 5, 2012 and May 6, 2013, respectively. (See Code of Civil Procedure §§ 335.1 and 338.) Plaintiff did not name Doe defendants in his previous complaints and therefore the proposed SAC does not relate back. (See Fireman's Fund Ins. Co. v. Sparks Const., Inc. (2004) 114 Cal.App.4th 1135, 1144, holding that failure to name Doe defendants pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure § 474 does not prevent a plaintiff from filing an amendment adding a new defendant; however, it does prevent the amendment from relating back.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/LCN)

rulings.Case Title: UN PLAZA LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. UN PLAZA ASSOCIATES, LP A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories And Rfps And Ntc Of Req. For Sanctions Of Attorneys'Fees Against Pltf
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, September 26, 2013, Line 5, DEFENDANTS UN PLAZA ASSOCIATES, L.P., HILL-UN PLAZA ASSOCIATES, INC.'S Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories And Rfps And Ntc Of Req. For Sanctions Of Attorneys'Fees Against Pltf The orders as follows: (1) While the parties did not fully satisfy their meet-and-confer obligations, the motion is ripe of decision. (2) Plaintiff UN Plaza LLC and Cross-Defendants Liberty Asses Management Corp. ("LAM"), Lucy Gao, and Benny Ko's objection based on the separate statements in support of the motion is overruled. (3) Plaintiff and Cross-Defendants' objections to the Nussbaum reply declaration are overruled. (4) Plaintiff and Cross-Defendants' objections that the discovery requests are not relevant are overruled. (5) Plaintiff and Cross-Defendants' objections based on trade secret and confidential information is overruled. (6) Plaintiff and Cross-Defendants' objections based on attorney work produce are overruled. (7) Plaintiff and Cross-Defendants are ordered to provide further responses to defendant's discovery requests as follows: Requests for production: to Plaintiff: Second Set, requests 6, 9, 11, 12, 14, 15, 16, 17; to LAM, Second Set, requests, 5, 7, 10, 12, 14, 15, 16, 17, 21, 22, 23, 24, 25, 26, 27, 28, 29; to Gao, Second set, requests, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 14, 16, 17, 21, 22, 23, 24, 25, 26, 27, 28, 29; to Ko, 11, 12, 14, 15, 16, 17, 21, 22, 23, 24, 25, 26, 27, 28, 29. Special Interrogatories: to LAM only, Interrogatories, 1, 4, 6, 7, 8, 9. (8) Plaintiff and Cross-Defendants are ordered to provide a privilege log with enough information for the parties, and if necessary, the Court, to assess the claim of privilege. (9) Supplemental document production and responses to document requests and interrogatories are to be made within 20 days after the Court's Order. (10) All parties' requests for sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ANNEX FINANCIAL, INC. VS. KATHERINE OBRIEN AKA KATHERINE S. OBRIEN AND ALSO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, September 26, 2013, Line 7, DEFENDANT KATHERINE OBRIEN'S MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT Defendant's motion to set aside default judgment is granted. Procedurally, the application was timely filed within 6 months of the date default was entered, and Defendant has attached a copy of the proposed answer to be filed. (CCP § 473(b).) Pursuant to the Court's discretionary powers under CCP § 437(b), the Court grants Defendant relief as the record shows that default judgment was entered against defendant as a result of her own mistake and inadvertence. (Id.) Plaintiff's request for $1000 in penalty fees under CCP § 473(c) is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: NEGINA MOHAMMAD VS. LITTLE CASEAR ENTERPRISES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, September 26, 2013, Line 10, DEFENDANT LITTLE CASEAR ENTERPRISES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff has had two opportunities to amend the complaint to state a cause of action. Plaintiff has not pled facts sufficient to establish the machine "impart[s] to the material some version of the die's own shape." (Rosales v. Depuy Ace Medical Co. (2000) 22 Cal.4th 279, 285.) Plaintiff has not pled facts sufficient to establish Defendant was "expressly informed by the manufacturer that a point of operation guard is required." (Award Metals, Inc. v. Superior Court (1991) 228 Cal.App.3d 1128, 1134.) The Court did not consider Plaintiff's extrinsic evidence - a demurrer can only be used to challenge defects that appear on the face of the pleading under attack or from matters outside the pleading that are judicially noticeable. (Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JANE ROE VS. DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Entry Of Protective Order Req. For Sanctions In The Amount Of $5000.00
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 26, 2013, Line 4: Defendants DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY'S, DEUTSCHE BANK AG'S, and NICOLE DESANTIS' Motion for Entry of Protective Order Request for Sanctions in the Amount of $5000.00 Pro Tem Judge James Weixel, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. Hearing required. The parties should be prepared to address the following issues in particular: 1) should a party producing documents or information be required to designate them as protected at the time of production, or should there be an automatic "hold" period; 2) should non-parties have standing under the protective order to designate documents or information as protected; 3) would the disclosure of the identity of an expert to whom protected materials are proposed to be disclosed conflict with the Code of Civil Procedure's requirements for expert disclosures and/or infringe on protections for attorney work product; and 4) who should bear the burden of justifying or challenging a designation that documents or information are protected. The issue of sanctions is reserved for the time of hearing. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CRISTINA CIUMAC VS. ALLSTATE INDEMNITY COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Reponses To Form Interrogatories, Sets One And Two; Req. For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 26, 2013, Line 5: Defendant GERMAN MOTORS COLLISION LLC'S Motion to Compel Further Reponses to Form Interrogatories, Sets One and Two; Request for Sanctions Pro Tem Judge James Weixel, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted in part and denied in part. It appears that plaintiff served "supplemental" (i.e., amended) responses to the set 1 and set 2 interrogatories on September 10, 2013. Accordingly, the motion to compel further responses is denied. However, defendant's request for monetary sanctions is granted in the amount of $1,200.00 per Cal. Rules of Court, rule 3.1348(a) and under Code of Civil Procedure section 2023.020 for plaintiff's failure to meet and confer. Plaintiff shall pay sanctions of $1,200.00, payable to defendant's counsel, within 30 days of the date of entry of this order. rulings.Case Title: KENNETH J. RADER JR VS. SUN LIFE ASSURANCE COMPANY OF CANADA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, September 26, 2013, Line 12, DEFENDANT DAVE JONES' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to allege that the Insurance Commissioner breached a specific mandatory duty. (Schwartz v. Poizner (2010) 187 Cal. App. 4th 572). A writ of mandate cannot be based on general enforcement provisions or statutes involving the defendant's exercise of discretion. Plaintiff also fails to allege facts showing that the defendant abused his discretion by acting arbitrarily. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/LCN)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT E. CARLONI VS. UNUM GROUP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, September 26, 2013, Line 13, DEFENDANT DAVE JONES' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to allege that the Insurance Commissioner breached a specific mandatory duty. (Schwartz v. Poizner (2010) 187 Cal. App. 4th 572). A writ of mandate cannot be based on general enforcement provisions or statutes involving the defendant's exercise of discretion. Plaintiff also fails to allege facts showing that the defendant abused his discretion by acting arbitrarily. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/LCN)

rulings.Case Title: COMMERCIAL INDUSTRIAL BUILDING OWNERS ALLIANCE, et al VS. SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, September 26, 2013, Line 14, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant San Francisco Unified School District's Demurrer to CIBA Plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint is sustained. As to the fourth cause of action (Negligence Against All Defendants), the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Common law negligence is not a permissible cause of action against a public entity. (Tolan v. State of California ex rel. Dep't of Transportation (1979) 100 Cal.App.3d 980, 986.) As to all other causes of action, the demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. Plaintiff has not pled with particularity every fact essential to the existence of statutory liability. (Susman v. City of Los Angeles (1969) 269 Cal.App.2d 803, 809; see also Mittenhuber v. City of Redondo Beach (1983) 142 Cal.App.3d 1, 5, "Because recovery is based on a statutory cause of action, the plaintiff must set forth facts in his complaint sufficiently detailed and specific to support an inference that each of the statutory elements of liability is satisfied. General allegations are regarded as inadequate.") The Court finds that the Complaint adequately pleads compliance with the presentation requirement of the Government Claims Act. (See First Amended Complaint, 5.) The Court did not consider the Declaration of John C. Ellis, as it was not attached to the FAC as an Exhibit, but was a separately filed document, and therefore, the facts alleged therein may not be considered on demurrer. (See Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318, holding that a demurrer only considers all material facts properly pleaded in the complaint and matters which may be judicially noticed.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/LCN)
